_note: CANON: the peoples of the world. 'cultures before races' — every entry leads with a WORLDVIEW, not a stat block. status:active = present in the game today; status:planned = written in the bible, not yet built. Enforced by scripts/world_coherence.mjs (every people needs a worldview). Content references peoples by id.

peoples.name: Goblins
peoples.worldview: Inventive, optimistic tinkerers who turn everyday life into adventure — every problem is a project, every failure a funnier prototype.

relations.rival: rival goblins (the Red-Ears) — same blood, opposite manners

peoples.name: Ember Imps
peoples.worldview: Friendly and enthusiastic, and accidentally set everything on fire — they mean well, which is precisely the danger.

peoples.name: Forest Sprites
peoples.worldview: Tiny caretakers of old forests who treat nature like family — a felled tree is a funeral, a seedling is a birth.

peoples.name: Moss Folk
peoples.worldview: Grow moss instead of hair, sleep for years, and think everyone else is unbearably impatient.

peoples.name: Boglings
peoples.worldview: Amphibious collectors who treasure polished junk and consider rust sacred — one goblin's scrap is a bogling's heirloom.

peoples.name: Pebblers
peoples.worldview: Living stone beings who speak slowly and measure time in seasons — a conversation may take a week and they consider that brisk.

peoples.name: Lantern Moths
peoples.worldview: Large nocturnal mothfolk who navigate by the stars and find darkness comforting — daylight is the frightening thing.

peoples.name: Root Keepers
peoples.worldview: Grow their homes instead of building them and negotiate with forests rather than cutting them — architecture is a conversation with something alive.
